commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=db69eca436bbc033a75e90375ec7efa8e689783d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes `config.openFile` failure responses and Control UI `openConfigFile` handling so failed opener attempts display an error and copy or show the config path.

PR surface: Source +22. Total +22 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: on current main the gateway returns `{ ok: false }` for opener failures while the UI only handles thrown requests. The linked headless VPS report provides the concrete `xdg-open: no method available` log path.
commands.review_followup: Keep this in normal PR review because the external contributor still needs to provide real behavior proof and the branch has narrow type/test blockers.

target.title: fix(ui): show actionable error feedback when config.openFile fails
target.body:
Fixes #87020

## Problem
Control UI shows no actionable feedback when `config.openFile` fails in a headless VPS environment:
- xdg-open fails with "no method available" error
- UI shows no error message to user
- Users may think the Control UI is broken

## Solution

### 1. Server Side Improvement
Enhanced error handling in `config.openFile` handler:
- Detect headless environment errors (`xdg-open` + `no method available`)
- Provide detailed, actionable error message:
  - "Cannot open file in headless environment"
  - File path included
  - Explanation of missing browser handler

### 2. Client Side Improvement
Enhanced `openConfigFile` in Control UI:
- Check response `{ ok: false }` properly
- Display error message via `state.lastError`
- Copy file path to clipboard as fallback
- Show actionable feedback to users

## Changes
- `src/gateway/server-methods/config.ts`: Improved error message formatting
- `ui/src/ui/controllers/config.ts`: Handle ok=false response properly

## Testing
Manual verification of error detection logic.

## Expected Behavior After Fix
When `config.openFile` fails in headless VPS:
1. UI shows error message
2. File path copied to clipboard automatically
3. User knows exactly what happened and has workaround
